name: Affordable Container Homes
description:
Affordable container homes from SAMAN are a 2,200 sq ft configured factory-built residence, delivered to your site at ₹36,66,000 — the same footprint that a mid-grade RCC contractor would price between ₹39.6 lakh and ₹48.4 lakh to build on your plot. The word "affordable" earns its place here when the number is specific: ₹1,665 per sq ft, factory-controlled quality, and 40 to 50 working days from order to keys in hand.

This page is written for the buyer who has a plot, has probably received at least one RCC quotation, and wants to know what ₹36,66,000 delivers in steel, insulation, interior finish, and usable floor area — and what that price does not reach. The build sheet comes first. The cost comparison follows. The excluded costs are listed plainly.

What the ₹36,66,000 Configuration Includes — 2,200 Sq Ft Build Sheet

SAMAN's affordable container home is a configured multi-level factory-built residence — 2,200 sq ft of insulated, finished floor area across two levels, built around an IS 2062 Grade A mild steel structural frame and factory-fitted with residential-grade insulation, electrical layout, flooring, and interior finish before it leaves our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility.

The frame is the part of this build that cannot be traded down without compromising the structure's 20-year lifespan. IS 2062 Grade A mild steel is the same structural specification used across our commercial container office units built for coastal and industrial sites. Surface treatment runs through blast cleaning to Sa 2.5, two coats of zinc-phosphate primer, and a polyurethane topcoat — total dry film thickness of 100 to 125 microns. The same treatment cycle applies to the structural connections between the two levels.

Wall panels are 50mm PUF sandwich panels — polyurethane foam pressed between two 0.50mm PPGI-coated steel skins. Thermal conductivity is approximately 0.022 W/m·K: interior temperatures run 8 to 10°C below ambient under direct sun, and a 1.5-ton split AC conditions the space comfortably at 42°C outdoor conditions. Roof insulation uses the same 50mm PUF panel construction — the overhead solar load in Indian summers is the heaviest heat input, so the roof gets identical treatment to the walls. The 50mm PUF wall panel specification is consistent across SAMAN's affordable shipping container homes range, from compact single-storey units to the 2,200 sq ft multi-level configuration on this page.

50mm PUF wall panel installation at SAMAN's container home fabrication facility. The panel edge reveals the white polyurethane foam core between PPGI steel skins — the same insulation specification used across every affordable container home produced on this line.

Floor construction is an 18mm structural base topped with vinyl plank finish. Doors are externally hung steel doors with mortise locks. Windows are sliding aluminium frames with single-glazed clear glass — single glazing is part of how this tier holds its price. Double glazing is available as an upgrade at ₹35,000 to ₹65,000 depending on window count, confirmed at the drawing stage.

The electrical layout covers wired switch and outlet points across both levels, an internal wiring loom sized for residential loads, and a main distribution board ready for the buyer's external mains connection. Light fittings and ceiling fans are buyer-supplied. Plumbing stub-outs at kitchen and bathroom positions are factory-fitted; buyer connects to water supply and drainage at the site.

For buyers who want single-unit procurement at a lower entry price, our prefab container homes range starts at ₹8.65 lakh per unit and covers both individual family homes and colony builds.

Is ₹36,66,000 Genuinely Affordable? The Cost-per-Sq-Ft Answer

At ₹36,66,000 for a 2,200 sq ft factory-built structure, a SAMAN affordable container home works out to approximately ₹1,665 per sq ft — below the ₹1,800–₹2,200 per sq ft typical of mid-grade RCC construction at the same footprint. The price includes frame, insulation, electrical, flooring, and factory delivery to your site.

That is the short answer. The longer answer involves what the two options look like side by side at the same floor area, including costs and timeline that the per-sq-ft number alone does not capture.

At 2,200 sq ft, a mid-grade RCC brick build in most Indian cities runs ₹39,60,000 to ₹48,40,000 in construction cost alone — before the civil foundation, before the 18 to 24 months of contractor coordination, and before the daily site-management overhead that comes with building a home of this size on a plot. The factory-built option lands below the lower end of that range as a configured, quality-checked unit.

Cost and time item RCC brick build, 2,200 sq ft SAMAN affordable container home
Construction / unit cost ₹39.6L to ₹48.4L (₹1,800–₹2,200/sq ft) ₹36,66,000 (configured base)
Civil foundation Included in construction estimate ₹1.0L to ₹1.8L (RCC plinth)
Time from order to move-in 18 to 24 months typical 40 to 50 working days
On-site disruption 18+ months of site activity Single crane-placement day
Quality control Site-dependent, contractor-specific Factory-controlled, identical output
Relocation potential None — permanent structure Possible with crane and trailer

SAMAN's 2,200 sq ft affordable container home on a semi-urban Indian residential plot. Two-storey factory-built structure with corrugated steel exterior in Burgundy Red and Forest Green — a family-scale residence at ₹1,665 per sq ft.

The construction line item is what surprises most buyers: the factory-built option comes out below mid-grade RCC cost per sq ft, and that advantage holds before time and coordination costs are added. For buyers building on a farmhouse plot, a hill-station property, or any site where 18+ months of active construction disruption is itself a cost, the container route is the value path even when the headline figures are close.

If your brief is premium designer interiors, architect-customised layouts, or resort-style finishes rather than a cost-benchmarked family home, our luxury container house range covers that scope at a different specification and price point.

What 2,200 Sq Ft Delivers as a Family Home — BHK Layout Options

2,200 sq ft is a serious family footprint. Across two factory-built levels, this configured floor plan gives an Indian household genuinely livable space — not a compromise.

Living-dining area inside SAMAN's 2,200 sq ft affordable container home. Vinyl plank flooring, recessed LED lighting, and split AC are factory-installed. One corrugated steel accent wall in Burgundy Red confirms the container structure. Standard Indian residential quality — realistic for the configured price tier.

A standard 3BHK layout at this footprint works as follows: three bedrooms averaging 160 to 180 sq ft each (480 to 540 sq ft combined), two attached bathrooms and one common bathroom at 45 to 55 sq ft each (135 to 165 sq ft combined), a living-dining area of 350 to 400 sq ft, a kitchen at 130 to 150 sq ft, a utility and storage area at 80 to 100 sq ft, and staircase and circulation space at 160 to 200 sq ft. That totals comfortably within 2,200 sq ft with room for a balcony on the upper level — included in the configured arrangement.

A 2BHK layout at the same footprint gives each bedroom 200 to 240 sq ft — larger than a typical 3BHK bedroom in an Indian city apartment — with a dedicated study or home office, an expanded living-dining at 450 sq ft, and a full-width kitchen. Buyers building for a couple with a child, or as a farmhouse anchor for extended-family stays, often find the 2BHK format with expanded common areas more comfortable than a compressed 3BHK.

Affordable modular container homes at this floor area are uncommon in the Indian market. Most competitors offer compact single-unit configurations or site-assembled arrangements with inconsistent quality control. The factory-built 2,200 sq ft unit from SAMAN is produced on the same facility floor as our commercial and industrial container products — the same frame tolerances, the same insulation press, the same QC checklist.

Door and window positions, internal partition placement, and room labelling are confirmed on a layout drawing during the design alignment stage — five working days after order confirmation, before factory fabrication begins.

What the Factory Price Does Not Cover — Site Costs for a 2,200 Sq Ft Build

Buyers who plan a container home successfully are the ones who know what is in the price and what is not before they sign. The ₹36,66,000 factory price covers the build, road transport from our nearest facility, crane placement on a buyer-prepared foundation, and a 5-year structural warranty on the steel frame. It does not cover the following.

RCC plinth foundation under preparation at a residential plot before SAMAN affordable container home delivery. A reinforced concrete pad of the correct dimensions — specified in SAMAN's structural drawings — is the buyer's site responsibility. Typical cost: ₹1.0 lakh to ₹1.8 lakh depending on ground conditions and local rates.

Foundation plinth. A multi-level 2,200 sq ft unit requires a reinforced RCC plinth sized for the full building load. Typical cost runs ₹1.0 lakh to ₹1.8 lakh depending on your local material rates, ground conditions, and the site contractor's rates. Our structural drawings, supplied with the order, specify the plinth dimensions and load-bearing requirements. The site needs single-vehicle trailer access for delivery and open clearance alongside the plinth for the crane to swing.

Electrical mains connection. The unit arrives with internal wiring complete and a distribution board ready for connection. The external service cable, meter tie-in, and coordination with your state electricity board are the buyer's scope — standard for any residential construction, factory-built or conventional.

Water supply and drainage. Plumbing stub-outs are factory-fitted at kitchen and bathroom positions. The connection from those stubs to your water source and to a sewage line or septic system is arranged at the site by the buyer or their local plumber.

Municipal approvals. A building plan sanction from your local authority is required before installation — BBMP in Bangalore, MCD in Delhi, or the equivalent body in your city. The approval process is identical to any new residential construction on your plot. No special approval category exists for container homes under the National Building Code.

Delivery beyond the standard zone. Our standard delivery zone covers most of peninsular India from the Bangalore facility and most of North India from Greater Noida. Sites beyond this radius carry a per-kilometre transport surcharge, quoted at the enquiry stage once we have your delivery pin code.

Affordable vs Inexpensive Container Homes — Which Footprint Fits Your Brief?

SAMAN makes two configured factory-built homes that serve the cost-driven buyer. The choice comes down to one question: how much floor area does your household need?

The 1,360 sq ft inexpensive option is priced at ₹25,35,000 — the right choice when your priority is the lowest configured price-point in the range that still delivers a full residential build sheet. It suits a couple or compact family, a solo farmhouse, or a stay unit for personal use. If you want the lowest entry cost and 1,360 sq ft covers your requirement, that page is the right starting point.

This page — the affordable 2,200 sq ft configuration at ₹36,66,000 — is the right choice when your household needs genuine family-scale floor area and you want that floor area priced competitively against what a contractor would quote for the same footprint in brick. Affordable is a comparison-to-RCC framing at a larger scale. Inexpensive is a price-floor framing at a smaller scale. Both are factory-built to the same engineering discipline, delivered to the same standard, and backed by the same frame warranty. The decision is a space decision — not a quality decision.

For buyers who need multiple units — project staff housing, resort clusters, or institutional accommodation — our prefab container homes range covers single-unit and colony procurement starting at ₹8.65 lakh per unit.

From Drawing Sign-Off to Move-In — The Order and Delivery Process

Once the order is confirmed, the 2,200 sq ft unit moves through three stages with no site construction activity until the final crane placement day.

Stage one — drawing and design alignment (5 working days). Door positions, window locations, internal partition placement, and electrical point positions are confirmed on a detailed layout drawing. No factory work begins before this sign-off. Changes after drawing confirmation may affect the fabrication timeline.

Stage two — factory fabrication (25 to 35 working days). The IS 2062 Grade A steel frame is welded and surface-treated. Wall and roof panels are pressed and fitted. The electrical loom is installed to the confirmed layout. Floor finish, staircase fabrication, and balcony structure are completed within the factory. For a 2,200 sq ft multi-level unit, factory build runs 25 to 35 working days depending on the customisation scope agreed at the drawing stage.

Crane placement day for a SAMAN affordable container home at an Indian residential site. The factory-built module is lifted from the delivery vehicle and positioned onto the prepared RCC plinth — single-day site activity, no extended construction period.

Stage three — dispatch, transport, and site placement (5 to 10 working days). The unit is road-transported from our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility. At the site, crane placement on the prepared plinth takes one working day. From drawing sign-off to move-in, total elapsed time runs 35 to 50 working days for this configuration. Complex customisation or remote delivery locations sit at the upper end.

For the full range of container house options across all price tiers — from compact single-unit configurations at ₹9.25 lakh to multi-level family homes — the container house overview covers every product in the cluster.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does an affordable container home cost in India?

A SAMAN affordable container home is priced at ₹36,66,000 for a 2,200 sq ft factory-built configured residence. This covers the IS 2062 Grade A steel frame, 50mm PUF insulated wall and roof panels, residential electrical layout, vinyl plank flooring, steel doors, aluminium-framed windows, and road transport from our facility. Site preparation costs — foundation plinth, mains electrical connection, and water and drainage hookup — are arranged separately and typically add ₹1.0 lakh to ₹2.5 lakh depending on site conditions and location.

Do affordable container homes need a building permit in India?

Yes. A container home on your plot follows the same approval process as conventional residential construction. You need a building plan sanction from your local municipal authority — BBMP in Bangalore, MCD in Delhi, or the equivalent local body in your city. The National Building Code does not impose a special restriction on container homes as permanent residential structures. If your land is approved for residential use and meets local setback requirements, the approval path is identical to any new residential build. Our structural drawings, supplied with the order, support the plan submission process.

Can I get a 2-bedroom or 3-bedroom affordable container home?

Yes. The 2,200 sq ft configured floor plan supports a full 3BHK layout — three bedrooms, two attached bathrooms, a common bathroom, living-dining, kitchen, and utility space — or a spacious 2BHK with larger individual rooms and an expanded living area. Internal layout, door positions, and room allocation are confirmed on a drawing during the five-working-day design alignment stage that opens the order. Buyers searching for 2 bedroom affordable container homes will find this 2,200 sq ft configuration is one of the few factory-built options in India that genuinely delivers that footprint at a sub-RCC price point.

What is the structural lifespan and warranty on an affordable container home?

A SAMAN container home built to IS 2062 Grade A mild steel specification, blast-cleaned to Sa 2.5, primed with zinc-phosphate, and topcoated with polyurethane carries a 5-year structural warranty on the steel frame and has a design lifespan of 20 to 25 years minimum under normal Indian weather and humidity conditions. Exterior repainting every 5 to 7 years and periodic inspection of door and window seals are the primary maintenance requirements. The 50mm PUF insulation panels have no moving parts and do not degrade under normal residential use.

Ready to Discuss Your Project?

Call or WhatsApp our team with your plot location and basic site details — we confirm delivery feasibility and send a full quotation within one working day.

+91 9708989937  |  WhatsApp: +91 9708989937
